														</table>
														<!--[if mso]>
                    </td>
                    <td valign="top" style="width:70%;">
                    <![endif]
                -->
														<table class="force-row" align="right" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" border="0" style="display:inline-table;float:right;width:528px;">
															<tr>
																<td class="content text" valign="top" style="
                                background-color:transparent;
                                padding-bottom:0px;
                                padding-left:0px;
                                padding-right:0px;
                                padding-top:0px;">
																	<table class="force-row" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" border="0" style="width:100%;">
																		<tr>
																			<td class="section_heading" valign="top" style="
                                                background-color:transparent;
                                                text-align:left;
                                                color:#003f2d;
                                                font-family:Tahoma, sans-serif;
                                                font-style:normal;
                                                font-weight:bold;
                                                font-size:19px;
                                                padding-bottom:0px;
                                                padding-left:0px;
                                                padding-right:0px;
                                                padding-top:0px;
                                                word-break:break-word;
                                            ">Investment Highlights</td>
																		</tr>
																		<tr>
																			<td valign="top" style="
                                                    line-height:5px;
                                                    height:5px;
                                                "> 
                                            </td>
																		</tr>
																		<tr>
																			<td class="copy" valign="top" style="
                                            color:#435254;
                                            font-family:Tahoma, sans-serif;
                                            font-size:13px;
                                        "><div><strong>6.6% In-Place Cap Rate & 75% LTV Assumable Fixed Rate Debt at 4.54% </strong><br>Offered at a low basis, Park at Greenhill delivers a 6.6% in-place T12 cap rate. The existing $43.6M Fannie Mae loan (originated through Greystone) is assumable at a fixed 4.54% interest rate with a 30-year amortization, currently interest-only through June 2027. At 75% LTV, the financing structure provides significant leverage at well below current market rates, reducing equity requirements and improving day-one cash-on-cash returns. <br><br><strong>Proven Value-Add with Remaining Upside - 7.1% Forecast Cap Rate</strong><br>The current owner has renovated a total of 166 units. Four of the most recently renovated units are commanding an average monthly rent premium of $161. The remaining 278 classic and 162 partially renovated units could be upgraded byt the new owner. Additional value-add levers a new owner can take advantage of include installing washer/dryer connections, charging a fee to rent 3 carports, converting prior basketball court to a sports court, and converting 2 unused buildings into rentable studio units or another amenity. <br><br><strong>Under Market Rents</strong><br>At an average effective rent of ~$1,156/month, the one- and two-bed unit styles at Park at Greenhill are currently running approximately 7-17% below the comps. The discount widens against the most directly comparable properties: the property is $226/month (17%) below 10 West, $131/month (10%) below Windsor Park, and $85/month (7%) below Brickyard Flats and The Hills. Even Boardwalk at Westlake — a 1,381-unit community built in 1965 with a mix that includes studios — achieves $1,085/month. Park at Greenhill’s own blended market rent of ~$1,177/month reflects $73/month of additional upside above current effective rents.<br><br><strong>Well-Maintained Asset with $6.3M+ Invested Since 2020</strong><br>The current ownership has invested over $6.3M in capital improvements since 2020. Significant spend includes $3.4M in unit turns and interior renovations, $974K in MEP (plumbing, HVAC, electrical), $859K in building envelope and structure (doors/windows, exterior painting, roofs), and $537K in site/common area improvements (paving, pool, concrete).  <br><br><strong>Distinctive Community Drives Quality Tenancy</strong><br>Park at Greenhill offers a rare indoor pool alongside a fitness center, soccer field, two playgrounds, dog park, grilling/picnic area, package lockers, and detached garages. The community is a mix of flats and townhomes spread across 51 buildings at under 15 units per acre, with private unit entrances. The unit mix includes rare 4-bedroom and 5-bedroom options — floor plans that are virtually nonexistent in the competitive set. Tenant quality is reinforced by a 3x rent-to-income qualification standard and a strong resident retention rate of 68%.<br><br><strong>Unique HOA/Condo Structure with Stable Income</strong><br>In 2006, 78 units were significantly renovated as part of a condo conversion strategy. Today, the owner LP holds 68 of those condo-unit interests as normal rental apartments, while 10 units were sold to outside investors who retain ownership but pay $387/month in HOA dues — generating approximately $46,440/year in stable, low-maintenance income. The property encompasses 78 total parcels (51 buildings plus individual units), with easements and HOA governance in place. The 10 individually-owned condos continue to have access to property amenities.<br><br><strong>Infill Location with Strong Demand Drivers</strong><br>The property sits in a dense infill corridor on the west side of Indianapolis, directly accessible from I-465 and within minutes of Indianapolis International Airport — a significant logistics and employment hub. The area’s workforce is heavily oriented toward transportation, warehousing, retail, and healthcare — a strong renter base. The property is also approximately 3 miles from the Indianapolis Motor Speedway and provides easy access to Downtown Indianapolis. A median age of 35.5 and a population that skews toward Millennials and Gen Z (51%) further supports sustained rental demand.​​​​​</div></td>
																		</tr>
																	</table>
																</td>
															</tr>
														</table>

                <![endif]-->

												</tr>
